organizations.title: Organizations
organizations.limitReachedTitle: Plan limit reached ({{max}}). Upgrade to add more organizations.

limitReachedNotice.title: Plan organization limit reached
limitReachedNotice.description_one: Your plan allows up to {{max}} active organization (single-tenant). Deactivate an existing organization or upgrade your plan to add more.
limitReachedNotice.description_other: Your plan allows up to {{max}} active organizations. Deactivate an existing organization or upgrade your plan to add more.

organizations.autoManagedNote: Auto-managed organizations (Sophos) can't be bulk-deactivated — use the Partner flow to manage them.

list.deleteAutoManagedTitle: Auto-managed organizations can only be removed via Partner deactivation
list.moreResultsNotice: Showing the first {{pageSize}} — refine the filters to see more.

empty.filteredTitle: No organization for the applied filters
empty.filteredDescription: Adjust the filters or clear them to see more results.
empty.defaultDescription: Create your first organization to get started.

bulkDeactivateModal.title: Deactivate {{count}} organization(s)?
bulkDeactivateModal.description: This action marks the selected organizations as inactive. Linked integrations keep existing, but the org disappears from default lists.
bulkDeactivateModal.autoIgnoredNote: Auto-managed and already-inactive organizations are automatically ignored by the server.

deleteDialog.title: Delete organization?
deleteDialog.description: Are you sure you want to delete {{name}}? Linked integrations will also be removed.

errors.bulkPartial: Bulk completed with warnings: {{deactivated}}/{{processed}} deactivated. {{errorsCount}} item(s) skipped.

users.title: Users
users.description: Manage portal users' access, roles and status.

feedback.userDeleted: User deleted. Action logged to the audit log.

empty.title: No user registered
empty.description: Create the first account to start administering the portal.
empty.searchDescription: No results for "{{query}}". Adjust the search term.

search.ariaLabel: Search users by name or username

deleteDialog.title: Delete user
deleteDialog.description: Delete {{name}}? This action is logged to the audit log and cannot be undone.

newUserModal.title: New user
newUserModal.organizationHelp: Non-admin users can only see data from their own organization. Admin is global.

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 10 characters
errors.organizationRequired: Select an organization for non-admin users

editUserModal.title: Edit user data
editUserModal.newPasswordHelp: Optional. Fill in only to reset.

errors.passwordTooShort: Password must be at least 10 characters
errors.organizationRequired: Non-admin users need an organization (without it they see no data)

editUserRoleModal.title: Edit user role
editUserRoleModal.reasonPlaceholder: E.g.: promoted to engineer in sprint 4...

admin.label: Admin
admin.description: Full access — users, configuration, all actions.

rolePermissionsViewer.title: Permissions matrix
rolePermissionsViewer.description: Overview of which permissions each role has in the system.

serviceAccounts.title: Service Accounts
serviceAccounts.description: Service Accounts are 'robot' identities without a session cookie. Use them for integrations that need a stable, revocable credential independent of any human (cron jobs, IASOC worker, etc).

empty.title: No Service Account
empty.description: Create a SA to authenticate workers and cron jobs without depending on a specific human's account.

deleteDialog.title: Delete Service Account
deleteDialog.description: Delete "{{name}}"? All {{count}} active tokens will be revoked in cascade. The audit log preserves the history.

feedback.deleted: Service Account "{{name}}" deleted. Its tokens were revoked in cascade.

createModal.title: New Service Account
createModal.nameHelp: Appears as sa:<name> in the audit log.
createModal.roleHelp: Defines the permission ceiling for tokens bound to this SA. Least-privilege principle: prefer viewer or operator.

editModal.title: Edit {{name}}
editModal.roleHelp: Changing role redefines the permission ceiling for existing tokens — without revoking them.
editModal.deactivateWarning: Deactivating invalidates all of this SA's tokens immediately (next request returns 401). Reactivating doesn't revive already-revoked tokens — issue new ones.

empty.title: No token issued
empty.description: This Service Account doesn't have any tokens yet. Create a new token to get started.

revokeDialog.title: Revoke token
revokeDialog.description: Revoke "{{name}}"? Integrations using this token will get 401 immediately.

createTokenModal.copyNowDescription: It won't be shown again. If you lose it, revoke it and create a new one.

tokens.title: Personal Access Tokens
tokens.description: Use Personal Access Tokens (PATs) to authenticate non-browser integrations (scripts, CI, pipelines). Each token inherits your current permissions and can be revoked at any time.

empty.title: No token created
empty.description: Create your first Personal Access Token to authenticate non-browser integrations.

revokeDialog.title: Revoke token
revokeDialog.description: Revoke the token "{{name}}"? Integrations using this token will start getting 401 immediately. This action is logged to the audit log.

createModal.copyNowDescription: For security reasons, this token won't be shown again. If you lose it, you'll have to revoke it and create a new one.
createModal.nameHelp: Use a name that identifies where the token will run — helps with auditing and rotation.
createModal.eternalWarningDescription: — we recommend setting a date. Eternal tokens are a preferred target in historical leaks.
